items.name: 輪西屯田兵旧火薬庫
items.location: 宮の森町(中嶋神社境内)
items.description: 明治19年（1886年）、第一回屯田兵村建設時に中隊本部とともに建てられた。

en.name_en: Former Powder Magazine of the Wanishi Tonden Colonist Soldiers
en.description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of the former powder magazine of the Wanishi tonden colonist soldiers. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Muroran City, designated on 1972-03-23, located within the precincts of Nakashima Shrine, Miyanomori-cho.

items.name: 輪西屯田兵記念碑
items.location: 宮の森町(中嶋神社境内)
items.description: 明治42年（1909年）、屯田兵中隊本部の記念碑が建立された。

en.name_en: Wanishi Tonden Colonist Soldiers Memorial Monument
en.description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of a memorial monument to the Wanishi tonden colonist soldiers. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Muroran City, designated on 1972-03-23, located within the precincts of Nakashima Shrine, Miyanomori-cho.

items.name: 輪西屯田兵関係資料
items.location: 宮の森町(中嶋神社社務所)
items.description: 中隊本部が使用していた印鑑、軍服等

en.name_en: Materials Relating to the Wanishi Tondenhei (Colonist-Soldiers)
en.description_en: This is a folk cultural property consisting of materials related to the Wanishi tondenhei (agricultural colonist-soldiers). It is municipally designated in Muroran City, where it is held at Miyanomori-chō (Nakajima Shrine office), designated on 23 March 1972.

items.name: 仙台藩角田領添田家関係資料
items.description: 明治3年（1870年）に仙台から入植した添田龍吉らは室蘭開拓の事業に当たった。その時に持参した刀、陣羽織等。（第1回指定昭和47年3月23日、第2回指定昭和48年3月27日。指定年月日は第1回指定日を採用）

en.name_en: Materials Relating to the Soeda Family of the Kakuda Domain of the Sendai Clan
en.description_en: This is a folk cultural property consisting of materials related to the Soeda family of the Kakuda territory of the Sendai domain. It is municipally designated in Muroran City, where it is held at Jinya-chō (Folk Materials Museum), designated on 23 March 1972.

items.name: 室蘭神楽
items.location: 海岸町(室蘭神楽保存会)
items.description: 明治34年（1901年）、新潟県の三条神楽が室蘭八幡宮に奉納され、今日まで伝承されている。昭和初期に、ご当地神楽「鯨神の舞」が創作された。

en.description_en: This is a folk cultural property, the Muroran kagura sacred performance. It is municipally designated in Muroran City, maintained at Kaigan-chō (Muroran Kagura Preservation Society), designated on 7 May 1998.

items.name: 御供獅子舞
items.location: 香川町(室蘭市香川町郷土保存会)
items.description: 明治25年（1892年）に香川県から入植した開拓者によって始められ、今日まで受け継がれている。

en.description_en: This is a folk cultural property, the Gokū shishimai (lion dance). It is municipally designated in Muroran City, maintained at Kagawa-chō (Muroran City Kagawa-chō Local Preservation Society), designated on 7 May 1998.

items.name: 瑞泉鍛刀所の鞴(ふいご)
items.location: 茶津町(日本製鋼所室蘭製作所内瑞泉鍛刀所)
items.description: 堀井家歴代の刀匠は、明治28年（1895年）に製作されたこの用具を使って、数々の名刀を生み出してきている。

en.name_en: The Bellows (Fuigo) of the Zuisen Swordsmithy
en.description_en: This is a folk cultural property, the bellows (fuigo) of the Zuisen swordsmithy. It is municipally designated in Muroran City, located at Chatsu-chō (Zuisen Swordsmithy within the Muroran Works of Japan Steel Works), designated on 2 November 2000.

items.name: 石川家不動明王像
items.description: 旧角田領主石川氏が奉じていたものと伝えられる。高さ51.5センチメートル(台座含む)、幅約24～31センチメートル、厚さ約18センチメートル。

en.name_en: The Ishikawa Family Statue of Fudō Myōō (Acala)
en.description_en: This is a folk cultural property, the Ishikawa family statue of Fudō Myōō (Acala). It is municipally designated in Muroran City, located at Kamiyo-chō (Jizō Hall), designated on 9 January 2003.

items.name: 仙台藩角田領添田家関係資料
items.description: 室蘭開拓の経緯等を記した古文書

en.name_en: Materials Relating to the Soeda Family of the Kakuda Domain of the Sendai Clan
en.description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of materials related to the Soeda family of the Kakuda territory of the Sendai domain. It is municipally designated in Muroran City, held at Motomachi (Municipal Library), designated on 7 May 1998.

items.name: 国産第1号航空機エンジン
items.location: 茶津町(日本製鋼所室蘭製作所)
items.description: （室0号。画像alt:日本製鋼所室蘭製作所製造 複葉機エンジン「室0号」）名称は原文で「有形文化財」とのみ記載。国産第1号の航空機エンジンで室蘭の企業が大正7年（1918年）に完成させた。

en.name_en: Japan's First Domestically Produced Aircraft Engine
en.description_en: This is a tangible cultural property, described as Japan's first domestically produced aircraft engine. It is municipally designated in Muroran City, located at Chatsu-chō (Muroran Works of Japan Steel Works), designated on 2 September 1999.

items.name: 南部藩陣屋跡出土砲弾
items.description: 国指定史跡東蝦夷地南部藩陣屋跡モロラン陣屋跡の内陣から出土した鉄製の球形砲弾。

en.name_en: Cannon Shells Excavated from the Nanbu Clan Jinya Site
en.description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of cannon shells excavated from the site of the Nanbu domain jinya (encampment). It is municipally designated in Muroran City, held at Jinya-chō (Folk Materials Museum), designated on 3 March 2010.

items.name: 胆振国室蘭郡全図
items.description: 明治初年に製作されたと推測される絵図。当時の地名や産物などを記載。

en.name_en: Complete Map of Muroran District, Iburi Province
en.description_en: This is a tangible cultural property, a complete map of Muroran district in Iburi Province. It is municipally designated in Muroran City, held at Motomachi (Municipal Library), designated on 3 March 2010.

items.name: 青い目の人形（友情人形）
items.description: 昭和2年（1927年）日米友好親善のためアメリカから贈られた人形。

en.description_en: This is a tangible cultural property, a blue-eyed doll (friendship doll). It is municipally designated in Muroran City, held at Jinya-chō (Folk Materials Museum), designated on 3 March 2010.

items.name: 白鳥大橋海底部等出土の材化石及び貝化石の標本
items.category: 記念物(史跡・名勝・天然記念物)
items.location: 祝津町(みたら室蘭)、陣屋町(民俗資料館)、本町(青少年科学館)
items.description: これらの化石を調査したところ、室蘭港はかつて湖であったことなど、3万9千年前以降における室蘭の地形の形成過程が明らかになった。

en.name_romaji: Hakuchō Ōhashi Kaiteibu tō Shutsudo no Zaikaseki oyobi Kaikaseki no Hyōhon
en.name_en: Specimens of Wood Fossils and Shell Fossils Excavated from the Seabed of the Hakuchō Bridge Area
en.description_en: This is a monument (natural monument) consisting of specimens of wood fossils and shell fossils excavated from the seabed and other areas of the Hakuchō Bridge. It is municipally designated in Muroran City, held at Shukuzu-chō (Mitara Muroran), Jinya-chō (Folk Materials Museum) and Motomachi (Youth Science Museum), designated on 2 November 2000.
